req.title: Bulk import employees
req.statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers bulk CSV import with per-row validation and partial commit semantics. I derive tests across five heuristics: (1) EP-positive: a well-formed CSV with all valid rows commits all rows and returns no errors; (2) NEG: uploading a non-CSV file triggers HTTP 415; (3) NEG/DT: uploading a CSV missing required header columns triggers HTTP 422; (4) BVA: a mixed CSV with exactly one valid row and one invalid row commits only the valid row and reports exactly one error, exercising the boundary between full-success and full-failure; (5) STC: the system transitions from 'no employees' through 'import in progress' to 'employees present', verified by checking state before and after a successful import. Each test is self-contained using fixtures for auth and ephemeral CSV construction, and cleans up imported records via a teardown call.

tests.name: test_bulk_import_all_valid_rows_committed
preconditions: Target employees do not already exist (idempotency guard)
CSV file contains only fully valid rows with all required columns
actions: Build an in-memory CSV with header row and 3 fully valid employee rows
POST multipart/form-data with the CSV to /employees/import
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Each employee is retrievable via GET /employees
trace: R-WEB-097: error report generated (zero errors expected)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io
import csv

def test_bulk_import_all_valid_rows_committed(hr_admin_session, api_base_url, cleanup_employees):
    employees = [
        {"employee_id": "EMP-T001", "first_name": "Alice", "last_name": "Smith", "email": "alice@example.com"},
        {"employee_id": "EMP-T002", "first_name": "Bob",   "last_name": "Jones", "email": "bob@example.com"},
        {"employee_id": "EMP-T003", "first_name": "Carol", "last_name": "White", "email": "carol@example.com"},
    ]
    buf = io.StringIO()
    writer = csv.DictWriter(buf, fieldnames=["employee_id","first_name","last_name","email"])
    writer.writeheader()
    writer.writerows(employees)
    buf.seek(0)
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", buf, "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported"] == 3
    assert body["errors"] == []
    cleanup_employees([e["employee_id"] for e in employees])

tests.name: test_bulk_import_non_csv_file_returns_415
preconditions: File to upload has a .txt extension and text/plain MIME type
actions: Build a plain-text file payload (not CSV)
POST multipart/form-data with the text file to /employees/import
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error message indicating invalid file type
trace: R-WEB-097: error pathway 415 when file not CSV
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_bulk_import_non_csv_file_returns_415(hr_admin_session, api_base_url):
    payload = b"this is just plain text, not a csv file"
    files = {"file": ("data.txt", payload, "text/plain")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 415
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

tests.name: test_bulk_import_missing_required_header_returns_422
preconditions: CSV file is valid CSV but omits the required 'email' header column
actions: Build an in-memory CSV with only partial headers (missing 'email')
POST multipart/form-data with the CSV to /employees/import
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body identifies the missing required column
trace: R-WEB-097: error pathway 422 when header missing required columns
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io
import csv

def test_bulk_import_missing_required_header_returns_422(hr_admin_session, api_base_url):
    buf = io.StringIO()
    # Intentionally omit 'email' from headers
    writer = csv.DictWriter(buf, fieldnames=["employee_id", "first_name", "last_name"])
    writer.writeheader()
    writer.writerow({"employee_id": "EMP-T099", "first_name": "Dan", "last_name": "Brown"})
    buf.seek(0)
    files = {"file": ("bad_headers.csv", buf, "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "email" in error_text or "missing" in error_text or "required" in error_text

tests.name: test_bulk_import_mixed_csv_commits_only_valid_rows
preconditions: CSV contains exactly 1 valid row and exactly 1 invalid row (boundary: N=1 per partition)
Valid employee does not already exist
actions: Build a CSV with one fully valid row and one row with a malformed email
POST multipart/form-data with the CSV to /employees/import
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body reports exactly 1 row imported
Response body reports exactly 1 error identifying the invalid row
Valid employee is retrievable via GET /employees
Invalid employee is NOT present in the system
trace: R-WEB-097: error report generated per row
R-WEB-097: commit only rows that pass validation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io
import csv

def test_bulk_import_mixed_csv_commits_only_valid_rows(hr_admin_session, api_base_url, cleanup_employees):
    buf = io.StringIO()
    fields = ["employee_id", "first_name", "last_name", "email"]
    writer = csv.DictWriter(buf, fieldnames=fields)
    writer.writeheader()
    writer.writerow({"employee_id": "EMP-T010", "first_name": "Eve",  "last_name": "Valid",   "email": "eve@example.com"})
    writer.writerow({"employee_id": "EMP-T011", "first_name": "Mal",  "last_name": "Invalid", "email": "NOT_AN_EMAIL"})
    buf.seek(0)
    files = {"file": ("mixed.csv", buf, "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported"] == 1
    assert len(body["errors"]) == 1
    assert body["errors"][0]["row"] in (2, "EMP-T011")
    get_resp = hr_admin_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/employees/EMP-T010")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    bad_resp = hr_admin_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/employees/EMP-T011")
    assert bad_resp.status_code == 404
    cleanup_employees(["EMP-T010"])

tests.name: test_bulk_import_state_transitions_no_employees_to_imported
preconditions: Target employees EMP-T020 and EMP-T021 do not exist (state: absent)
actions: Verify employees are absent via GET /employees/{id} returning 404 (state: absent)
Verify employees are present via GET /employees/{id} returning 200 (state: imported)
expected: Import response HTTP 200 or 201 with 2 imported, 0 errors
trace: R-WEB-097: state transition absent -> present after successful import
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io
import csv

def test_bulk_import_state_transitions_no_employees_to_imported(hr_admin_session, api_base_url, cleanup_employees):
    ids = ["EMP-T020", "EMP-T021"]
    for eid in ids:
        pre = hr_admin_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/employees/{eid}")
        assert pre.status_code == 404, f"Precondition failed: {eid} already exists"
    buf = io.StringIO()
    fields = ["employee_id", "first_name", "last_name", "email"]
    writer = csv.DictWriter(buf, fieldnames=fields)
    writer.writeheader()
    writer.writerow({"employee_id": "EMP-T020", "first_name": "Frank", "last_name": "A", "email": "frank@example.com"})
    writer.writerow({"employee_id": "EMP-T021", "first_name": "Grace", "last_name": "B", "email": "grace@example.com"})
    buf.seek(0)
    files = {"file": ("state.csv", buf, "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    assert resp.json()["imported"] == 2
    for eid in ids:
        post = hr_admin_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/employees/{eid}")
        assert post.status_code == 200
    cleanup_employees(ids)
